question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum inflight request limit in MetricsMiddleware to open thousands of simultaneous connections, causing requestInflightGauge to reach values that trigger automated network partition decisions by orchestration systems?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with crafted Content-Length headers that mismatch actual body size to cause requestBytesHistogram to record incorrect values, corrupting capacity planning metrics and causing operators to misconfigure network partitioning thresholds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that requestInflightGauge uses a GaugeVec (which can go negative if decremented more than incremented) to send crafted requests that cause the gauge to underflow, producing negative inflight counts that confuse circuit breaker logic?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET /metrics requests while simultaneously flooding other endpoints to create a lock contention scenario between promhttp.Handler and MetricsMiddleware metric updates, causing the server to deadlock and partition from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sizeBuckets definition (512, 1024, 10*1024, 25*1024, 50*1024) by sending requests just above 50*1024 bytes to consistently land in the +Inf overflow bucket, causing Prometheus alerting rules based on bucket ratios to fire false network partition alerts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that intentionally trigger 503 responses from the rosetta backend to inflate the ResponseCodeRatio tracked by Traefik's circuitBreaker above the 0.25 threshold, causing the circuit breaker to open and create a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the weaveworks middleware.Instrument's route matching logic by sending requests to non-existent routes, causing the RouteMatcher to return empty route labels and creating a high-cardinality unknown route label in requestInflightGauge that exhausts memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with HTTP/2 multiplexing to open hundreds of streams simultaneously, causing requestInflightGauge to spike beyond the Traefik inFlightReq limit of 5 per source IP, triggering request rejection that mimics a network partition for legitimate clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /metrics endpoint to read hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight values and determine when the node is most vulnerable to a targeted DoS that would cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with extremely long URL paths to create high-cardinality route labels in requestDurationHistogram, causing the Prometheus registry to allocate unbounded memory and eventually OOM-kill the rosetta process?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on MetricsMiddleware-instrumented endpoints to send a sustained flood of requests that keeps requestInflightGauge above the Traefik inFlightReq threshold of 5, permanently blocking all legitimate traffic?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the weaveworks middleware.Instrument to record a ws=true label in requestDurationHistogram by exploiting WebSocket upgrade headers, creating unexpected label combinations that corrupt duration metric aggregations used for network health decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the prometheus.DefaultRegisterer to become corrupted by triggering concurrent metric registration through parallel process startup, causing MustRegister to panic and preventing the rosetta node from initializing and joining the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with forged X-Forwarded-For headers to bypass the Traefik ipStrategy depth:1 rate limiting, allowing them to send more than 10 requests/second and overwhelm the metrics instrumentation layer, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MetricsMiddleware does not validate the route label value to inject newline characters into requestInflightGauge label values, corrupting the Prometheus text format output and causing scrape failures that mask network partition events?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /metrics request with a very large number of Accept-Encoding variants to cause promhttp.Handler to spend excessive time on content negotiation, delaying metric delivery to monitoring systems and causing false network partition alerts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the requestDurationHistogram bucket boundaries (.1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5 seconds) by crafting requests that consistently take just over 5 seconds to cause all observations to land in the +Inf bucket, triggering SLO violation alerts that cause operators to manually partition the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of requests to non-existent routes to cause the RouteMatcher to generate a unique route label per path, creating unbounded cardinality in responseBytesHistogram and exhausting Prometheus registry memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the middleware chain order (MetricsMiddleware -> TracingMiddleware -> CorsMiddleware) to send requests that are counted by MetricsMiddleware but rejected by CorsMiddleware, causing requestInflightGauge to increment without a corresponding response, permanently inflating the gauge?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with HTTP CONNECT method to cause the middleware.Instrument to record unexpected method labels in requestBytesHistogram, creating label combinations that were not anticipated in the design and causing metric collection to behave unexpectedly during a real network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /metrics endpoint to read hiero_mirror_rosetta_response_bytes histogram data and infer the size of internal API responses, enabling targeted attacks that maximize response sizes and exhaust network bandwidth to cause a partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the weaveworks middleware.Instrument to fail to decrement requestInflightGauge due to a panic in the downstream handler, causing the gauge to grow monotonically and eventually trigger automated network isolation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a circuit breaker within MetricsMiddleware itself to send a sustained attack that keeps the rosetta node busy processing metric instrumentation overhead, starving legitimate network consensus traffic and causing a de facto network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with duplicate or conflicting HTTP headers to cause the middleware.Instrument to record inconsistent request body sizes in requestBytesHistogram, corrupting capacity metrics and causing operators to misconfigure network partition thresholds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that requestInflightGauge is a global variable (package-level var) to cause a data race by sending concurrent requests that simultaneously read and write the gauge, leading to memory corruption and a process crash that partitions the node?
